title: How does QuickCrate ensure food safety standards?
body: QuickCrate maintains strict food safety protocols across its dark-store network. All dark stores handling food products are FSSAI-licensed and undergo periodic internal audits and surprise inspections by our quality team. Cold storage equipment is calibrated and monitored 24/7 with IoT temperature sensors — any deviation triggers an automatic alert to the store manager. Staff handling fresh produce, dairy, and meat alternatives are trained in food hygiene (hand-washing, glove use, surface sanitation) as per FSSAI guidelines. Products near expiry are flagged by our inventory system and removed from sale at least 3 days before the expiry date for perishables. Incoming stock from suppliers is inspected for packaging integrity, batch codes, and temperature on arrival. Our food safety records are available for regulatory review on request.

title: What should I do if I find a foreign object in a product?
body: Finding a foreign object (glass, plastic, metal, insect, etc.) in a food product is a serious safety issue. Stop consuming the product immediately. Report it via 'My Orders → Report Issue → Foreign Object Found'. Upload a clear photograph of the foreign object in the product. Do not discard the product — preserve it as evidence. Our food safety team will contact you within 2 hours. The incident triggers an immediate product lot quarantine and supplier audit. You'll receive a full refund and a replacement, as well as a formal safety investigation report once completed. If you believe there is a health risk from ingestion of the foreign object, seek medical attention immediately. QuickCrate will cover reasonable documented medical expenses related to product safety failures. We report serious incidents to FSSAI as required by law.

title: Are QuickCrate riders' food safety practices adequate?
body: Yes, QuickCrate's rider onboarding includes a mandatory food safety module covering proper handling of food parcels, temperature bag usage, and hygiene during contactless delivery. Riders are instructed to keep food parcels upright, not stack heavy non-food items on top of food bags, and to maintain personal hygiene (clean hands, clean bags). Thermal insulation bags used by riders are inspected and replaced on a schedule. Riders handling special dietary items (nut-free, allergen-sensitive) are briefed by the dark store on separation requirements. Rider compliance is monitored via customer ratings and periodic mystery shopper evaluations. If you ever observe a rider mishandling your order (e.g., opening packaging, incorrect temperature bag use), report it via 'Rate Order → Report Conduct Issue' so we can address it directly with the rider.

title: Does QuickCrate sell alcohol and tobacco products?
body: QuickCrate sells alcohol in states where online alcohol delivery is legally permitted (currently Maharashtra, Telangana, West Bengal, and select others as regulations evolve). Age verification is mandatory — you must be 21+ to purchase alcohol on QuickCrate. At checkout for alcohol products, you'll be prompted to confirm your age and upload a government-issued ID (Aadhaar, passport, driving license). The rider will also perform an age check at the door before handing over alcohol products. Tobacco and cigarettes are not listed on QuickCrate's platform due to COTPA advertising restrictions and minimum-age compliance complexities. E-cigarettes and vaping products are also not sold. QuickCrate reserves the right to refuse delivery of alcohol if the recipient appears to be a minor or is visibly intoxicated.

title: How does QuickCrate handle allergen information?
body: QuickCrate displays allergen information for packaged food products as provided by the manufacturer. On each product detail page, look for the 'Allergens' section under 'Product Info', which lists major allergens (gluten, nuts, dairy, soy, eggs, shellfish, etc.) in bold as per FSSAI labeling rules. For loosely packed or freshly prepared items, allergen info may be limited — exercise caution if you have severe allergies. You can filter products by allergen-free labels (e.g., 'Gluten-Free', 'Nut-Free') using the catalog filters. QuickCrate strongly recommends always reading the physical product label, as manufacturers may reformulate products without immediate updates to digital listings. QuickCrate is not liable for allergic reactions caused by undisclosed manufacturer changes or cross-contamination during production. Customers with life-threatening allergies should consult the manufacturer directly.

title: How does QuickCrate vet its suppliers?
body: QuickCrate has a rigorous supplier onboarding process. New suppliers must provide valid FSSAI licenses (for food), GST registration, and product quality certification (ISO, BIS, or equivalent). Brand-authorized letters are required for branded goods to ensure authenticity. Suppliers are also evaluated on delivery reliability, cold-chain compliance (for perishables), packaging standards, and pricing consistency. After onboarding, suppliers are subject to ongoing performance monitoring: customer return rates for their products, quality inspection at dark-store receipt, and periodic on-site audits of their production or warehousing facilities. Suppliers failing to meet standards are placed on probation and ultimately removed if issues persist. QuickCrate does not work with unregistered or unlicensed suppliers for food, health, or personal care products.

title: What is QuickCrate's policy on counterfeit products?
body: QuickCrate has a zero-tolerance policy for counterfeit products. We source all goods from brand-authorized distributors and do not knowingly list or sell counterfeit items. If you suspect a product you received is counterfeit (mismatched packaging, wrong holograms, unusual smell or appearance), report it immediately via 'My Orders → Report Issue → Suspected Counterfeit'. Do not use the product. Attach photos of the packaging, batch code, and the suspect feature. Our authentication team will verify the product against brand specifications within 48 hours. If confirmed counterfeit, you'll receive a full refund and the matter is reported to the brand and relevant authorities. The supplier is immediately suspended pending investigation. QuickCrate cooperates with brand protection teams and law enforcement on counterfeit investigations.

title: How does QuickCrate ensure rider safety on the road?
body: QuickCrate prioritizes rider safety through policy, training, and technology. All riders are required to wear helmets and reflective vests on every delivery. The QuickCrate rider app enforces speed limits — if a rider exceeds 60 km/h for more than 30 seconds, the incident is logged and reviewed by the safety team. Riders are trained to prioritize safety over delivery speed; QuickCrate's internal KPIs for riders include safety scores, not just delivery time. Riders can report unsafe road conditions (waterlogging, accidents, construction) through the rider app, which triggers automatic rerouting for subsequent deliveries. During extreme weather, QuickCrate proactively pauses or reduces deliveries. Rider insurance is provided by QuickCrate covering accident and medical expenses. Fatigue management policies restrict riders to 8 hours of active delivery time per day.

title: What are QuickCrate's quality standards for fresh produce?
body: Fresh produce at QuickCrate undergoes a multi-stage quality process. At the source (farms and wholesale markets), our procurement team inspects produce for freshness, size consistency, and absence of pest damage before purchase. Upon arrival at the dark store, all fresh items are re-inspected and any substandard pieces are removed before being stocked. The dark store maintains separate storage zones for fruits, vegetables, and leafy greens, each at optimal temperature and humidity levels. Produce is restocked daily or twice daily to minimize shelf time. Items approaching the end of freshness are offered at a discount ('Flash Deals') rather than being sold at full price. Customer feedback on freshness is tracked per batch, and persistent issues with a specific supplier are escalated. Our goal is that all fresh produce delivered has at least 48 hours of shelf life remaining.

title: How can I report a safety concern about a product or delivery?
body: QuickCrate provides multiple channels for reporting safety concerns. For product safety issues (contamination, foreign objects, expired product, suspected counterfeit, allergen mislabeling), use 'My Orders → Report Issue' to file a formal complaint with photo evidence. For delivery safety issues (reckless riding, intoxicated rider, damaged packaging from poor handling), use 'Rate Order → Report Conduct Issue' or contact support directly. For general safety concerns not tied to a specific order (e.g., you observed unsafe practices at a dark store), email safety@quickcrate.in. All safety reports are treated with high priority. You may remain anonymous for non-order-linked reports. QuickCrate is required by law to report certain food safety incidents to FSSAI and will do so without requiring your consent as a whistleblower. Your report helps keep the entire QuickCrate community safe.
